title: AMQP 0-9-1 Topology and Routing Models · Runtime Contract: Vhost Namespace Isolation
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must encode exchanges, bindings, routing keys, and zero-to-many destinations as deterministic Go values; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the vhost namespace isolation increment in an executable Go topology and destination model. Learners progress through pred…
objectives: Use virtual hosts as separate topology and permission namespaces while recognizing they are not complete process or resource isolation; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Trace producers, connections, channels, exchanges, bindings, queues, consumers, deliveries, and acknowledgements without collapsing their roles; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Vhost Namespace Isolation · Theory Vhost Namespace Isolation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 4771 during bounded worker pool during a dependency slowdown. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive zero, one, or many queue destinations from AMQP exchange and binding values; separate deterministic browser proof from race-enabled and live-broker evidence; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
